articles.title: A grave situation: Medway police seek return of 12-foot-tall skeleton’s head
articles.description:
“We’re not joking … and we’re definitely not okay,” Medway Parks and Recreation said.

The post A grave situation: Medway police seek return of 12-foot-tall skeleton’s head appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Medway police are investigating the theft of a 12-foot-tall skeleton's head.
The skeleton is part of a Halloween display by Medway Parks and Recreation.
Residents are concerned about community safety and the impact on local festivities.

articles.title: Sold out show? Not if you book at this Boston hotel.

articles.title: Six from N.H. charged in ‘brazen’ Downtown Crossing assault and robbery
articles.description:

summary: Six individuals from New Hampshire charged in Downtown Crossing assault on March 19.

Increased concerns for safety in Downtown Crossing area among Boston residents.

articles.title: Review: ‘Project Hail Mary’ borders on miraculous
articles.description:
Ryan Gosling's funny sci-fi adventure is one of the most purely entertaining blockbusters in recent memory, even though it doesn't quite stick the landing.

summary: Ryan Gosling stars in 'Project Hail Mary', a new sci-fi blockbuster released March 2026.

Despite mixed reviews, it contributes to Boston's vibrant film culture and cinema attendance.

articles.title: City Council calls for more transparency from Wu over transportation projects
articles.description:

summary: City Council demands increased transparency from Mayor Michelle Wu regarding stalled transportation projects.
Councilors express concerns that Wu's policies are delaying critical infrastructure developments in Boston.
Lack of communication may affect future funding and progress on essential transportation initiatives.

articles.title: ‘All the negativity that’s in this town sucks’: Revisiting Rick Pitino’s iconic Celtics rant
articles.description:
Pitino went 102-146 in three-and-a-half seasons, a tough outcome for someone who once wrote a book titled, “Success is a Choice.”

The post ‘All the negativity that’s in this town sucks’: Revisiting Rick Pitino’s iconic Celtics rant appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Rick Pitino's Celtics tenure ended with a 102-146 record over three and a half seasons.
Pitino's famous rant highlighted local frustrations, resonating with Boston fans on March 19, 2026.
His comments reflect ongoing challenges in Boston's sports culture and fan expectations.

articles.title: Patriots sign veteran cornerback Kindle Vildor
articles.description:
In New England, Vildor will round out the cornerbacks depth chart, competing with Charles Woods and Kobee Minor as the third perimeter option.

summary: Patriots sign cornerback Kindle Vildor to enhance defensive depth.
